claims.claim_text: SonarQube uses scanners, a server, stored metrics/issues, and quality gates to support code quality and security inspection.
claims.rationale: The official SonarQube analysis overview directly describes scanner analysis, report upload, server computation, quality gate calculation, and reports, but docs.sonarsource.com is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube for IDE/SonarLint connected mode can bind an IDE project to SonarQube Server and apply server-side quality profiles and settings locally.
claims.rationale: The connected mode documentation directly describes binding IDE projects to SonarQube and sharing quality profiles and settings, but the source host is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: SonarScanner analysis may need build outputs, coverage reports, branch or pull request metadata, and authentication tokens to upload analysis.
claims.rationale: SonarSource's analysis overview and related scanner docs cover scanner execution, report forwarding, branch/PR analysis, and authentication, but the official docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube Server runs Web, Elasticsearch, and Compute Engine processes, and its database stores metrics, issues, instance configuration, and the report queue.
claims.rationale: The Server components page directly lists these processes and database responsibilities, but docs.sonarsource.com is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: Pull request analysis can report a quality gate status and pull request decoration before merge.
claims.rationale: The official pull request analysis documentation directly covers PR analysis and quality gate status reporting, but the domain is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube metrics cover reliability, security, maintainability, coverage, duplication, and security review/security hotspot signals.
claims.rationale: The SonarQube metric definitions page lists these metric families, but the official documentation domain is not in the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Security hotspots differ from vulnerabilities because hotspots require human review before deciding whether a fix is needed.
claims.rationale: The Security Hotspots documentation directly distinguishes hotspots from vulnerabilities, but the official SonarSource docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube technical debt is based on remediation effort assigned to issues and can be aggregated by project or category.
claims.rationale: The metrics definition page explains remediation effort and technical debt aggregation, but docs.sonarsource.com is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: A quality gate is a set of metric conditions that returns pass or fail, and conditions can apply to new code or overall code.
claims.rationale: The quality gate introduction directly defines quality gates and new-code versus overall-code conditions, but the domain is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Sonar way's new-code quality gate conditions include all new security hotspots reviewed, new code coverage at least 80.0%, and new-code duplication at most 3.0%.
claims.rationale: The SonarQube quality gate docs list these exact built-in Sonar way conditions, but the official source is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube imports coverage reports generated by external tools rather than generating coverage itself.
claims.rationale: The official test coverage parameters page says coverage reports are generated by external tools and imported by SonarQube, but the docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube analysis parameters such as sonar.sources, sonar.tests, sonar.exclusions, and coverage report path properties define analysis scope and report import.
claims.rationale: SonarSource's analysis parameters page directly documents source, test, exclusion, and report path parameters, but it is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: For SonarQube monorepo analysis, teams can configure analysis parameters for each project and add separate jobs or tokens for projects.
claims.rationale: The GitHub Actions integration documentation describes monorepo setup with per-project analysis parameters, tokens, and jobs, but the domain is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: For GitHub Actions, SonarQube recommends disabling shallow clones with fetch-depth: 0, and sonar.qualitygate.wait makes the scanner wait for the quality gate with a default timeout of 300 seconds.
claims.rationale: The GitHub Actions integration page directly documents fetch-depth 0, qualitygate.wait, and the 300-second default timeout, but docs.sonarsource.com is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarSource/sonarqube-scan-action@v7.1.0 is a real released GitHub Action version.
claims.rationale: The GitHub release page directly identifies v7.1.0 as a released action version.

claims.claim_text: SonarSource/sonarqube-quality-gate-action@v1.2.0 is a real released GitHub Action version.
claims.rationale: The GitHub release page directly identifies v1.2.0 as a released quality gate action version.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube's GitLab CI guidance uses GIT_DEPTH: "0", scanner cache, sonarsource/sonar-scanner-cli, SONAR_TOKEN/sonar.token, and sonar.qualitygate.wait for quality-gate failure behavior.
claims.rationale: The GitLab CI integration page directly documents these settings and examples, but the official documentation host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Pull request decoration requires the relevant repository integration and pull request analysis support in the SonarQube edition in use.
claims.rationale: The pull request analysis setup page documents edition support and DevOps platform integration requirements, but the domain is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The SonarQube database stores metrics, issues, instance configuration, and report queue data, making backups and production operations important.
claims.rationale: The server components page directly lists SonarQube database responsibilities, but the source host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The sonarqube Docker Official Image is maintained for SonarQube Server and Community Build deployments.
claims.rationale: Docker Hub's official image page directly identifies the SonarQube image and its maintained purpose.

claims.claim_text: SonarQube on Linux requires vm.max_map_count to be at least 524288 for its Elasticsearch-backed search component.
claims.rationale: The official Linux pre-installation page gives the exact vm.max_map_count threshold, but docs.sonarsource.com is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: SonarSource maintains an official Helm chart repository for deploying SonarQube on Kubernetes.
claims.rationale: The GitHub repository README identifies it as the SonarSource Helm Chart for SonarQube.

claims.claim_text: The Helm values shown include a PostgreSQL dependency or embedded database suitable for a lab or small internal instance.
claims.anchor_text: A Helm deployment lets platform teams express the same intent with less handwritten YAML. The values below are intentionally conservative: one application replica for community-style deployment, persistent storage, a PostgreSQL dependency for a lab or small internal instance, ingress enabled, and authentication forced so anonymous users do not browse internal code metrics. Production teams should replace the embedded database with their standard PostgreSQL service when that is their organizational pattern.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A Helm deployment lets platform teams express the same intent with less handwritten YAML. The values below are intentionally conservative: one application replica, persistent storage, ingress enabled, and authentication forced so anonymous users do not browse internal code metrics. Production teams should connect SonarQube to their standard PostgreSQL service when that is their organizational pattern.
claims.rationale: Current SonarSource chart metadata and values do not show a PostgreSQL dependency, so the embedded/dependency wording should be removed rather than cited.

claims.claim_text: In most languages, //NOSONAR suppresses all SonarQube issues on that line, including future issues raised on the line.
claims.rationale: The issue management documentation explicitly describes //NOSONAR behavior, but docs.sonarsource.com is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Command execution through a shell with untrusted input is an OS command injection risk.
claims.rationale: MITRE CWE-78 directly documents OS command injection from externally influenced commands.

claims.claim_text: Weak hashing, such as using MD5 for password hashing, is a security finding.
claims.rationale: MITRE CWE-328 directly covers use of weak one-way hash functions.

further_reading.title: SonarQube
further_reading.why_relevant: Upstream repository overview for SonarQube's continuous inspection and quality gate role.

further_reading.title: SonarQube Scan Action
further_reading.why_relevant: Canonical GitHub Action repository for SonarQube scans in CI workflows.

further_reading.title: SonarQube Helm Chart
further_reading.why_relevant: Official chart source for Kubernetes deployment patterns used by platform teams.

notes: Fenced code blocks, ASCII diagrams, quiz answers, and routine exercise commands were generally excluded per instructions. SonarSource documentation is the authoritative source for most SonarQube-specific behavior, but docs.sonarsource.com is not in the current allowlist, so those claims are marked needs_allowlist_expansion.
